title: machine start: qemu: adjust backoffs
body:
Make sure that starting a qemu machine uses proper exponential backoffs and that a single variable isn't shared across multiple backoffs.

DO NOT BACKPORT: I want to avoid backporting this PR to the upcoming 4.6 release as it increases the flakiness of machine start (see #17403). On my M2 machine, the flake rate seems to have increased with this change and I strongly suspect that additional/redundant sleep after waiting for the machine to be running and listening reduced the flakiness.  My hope is to have more predictable behavior and find the sources of the flakes soon.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

yes I think all sleep() code is increasing flakiness so getting rid of them might fix the root cause (and having a way to get rid of them at a single place might help to find the root cause)

I want a fast start of a machine as well (so avoiding any unwanted/flaky wait is preferable)

Good idea but if the goal here is to address the flake (which only happens for the ssh volume mount???) maybe I can redirect your attention to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16143. This should really just be managed by systemd so that a user can reboot from within the VM without problems.

> Good idea but if the goal here is to address the flake (which only happens for the ssh volume mount???) 

There is a number of issues including the flaky SSH.  I think it's still valuable to identify why it's failing sometimes.  It does not always fail on the first mount attempt.

> maybe I can redirect your attention to #16143. This should really just be managed by systemd so that a user can reboot from within the VM without problems.

Thanks for the reminder.  I agree, that would be a nice solution.
Good to go from my side :heavy_check_mark:
